title: Question: Verilator instalation
body:
---


Author Name: **JoÃ£o Fernandes**
Original Redmine Message: 1098 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi everyone.

First let me thank the developers for this amazing tool.
Right now i think i am having some troubles with the usage of verilator, but i can't really find out the cause.
Verilator works fine if i don't use System C, i have made some simulations using only C++ that work as expected.
I have made the installation of all the components(Verilog-Perl,SystemPerl and Verilator) from git.

When doing the make check step on verilator folder the test_c does pass but the test_sc does also finish but in the end gives me this output:
```
obj_dir/simx

              SystemC 2.3.0-ASI --- May  9 2013 19:08:34
         Copyright (c) 1996-2012 by all Contributors,
         ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

Defining Clocks
Defining Interconnect
Test initialization...
Enabling waves...
Test beginning...
[3] t_param: Running
    1  2  3
 Params = 00000000000000000000000000100011 011
    00000000000000000000000000000010 00000000000000000000000000100011
[3] t_param: Passed
[3] t_chg: Running
[3] t_inst: MODE = 0  IS=1 OS=0
[3] t_inst: Running
[12] t_netlist: Passed
[13] t_chg: Passed
[13] t_inst: MODE = 1  IS=1 OS=0
[13] t_clk: Running
[23] t_inst: MODE = 2  IS=1 OS=0
[23] t_inst: Passed
[33] t_inst: MODE = 3  IS=1 OS=0
[43] t_inst: MODE = 3  IS=1 OS=0
[53] t_clk: Passed
[53] t_inst: MODE = 3  IS=1 OS=0
*-* All Finished *-*
*** glibc detected *** obj_dir/simx: double free or corruption (fasttop): 0x0000000001b0d220 ***
======= Backtrace: =========
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(+0x76d76)[0x2b3a06d0cd76]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(cfree+0x6c)[0x2b3a06d11aac]
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6(_ZNSsD1Ev+0x20)[0x2b3a06398b20]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(__cxa_finalize+0xa5)[0x2b3a06ccd175]
/usr/local/systemc-2.3.0/lib-linux64/libsystemc-2.3.0.so(+0x73013)[0x2b3a05fd5013]
======= Memory map: ========
...

```

Similar behavior happens to the simulations i have build (based on those makefile) that use systemc, at the end they give this error.

I am using gcc version 4.7.2 (Debian 4.7.2-5), and Debian EGLIBC 2.13-38 and Verilator 3.847 devel rev verilator_3_846-10-g54eedcc

The Verilog-Perl and SystemPerl all passed the instalation checks.

Best regards,

JoÃ£o Fernandes
